summary: Phase 3 (Google Drive export integration) — full backend + frontend verification. Backend: 44/44 pytest pass: 15 new tests in test_drive_export.py + 29 regression. Drive status/connect/callback/disconnect/export endpoints all enforce auth (401), validate ownership (403), 404 on missing post, and 400 with detail 'not connected' when Drive not linked. The Google authorization URL is properly built: starts with https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth, embeds client_id 988059460414…, scope=https%3A//www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file (URL-encoded), redirect_uri=https://tani-share.preview.emergentagent.com/drive/callback, response_type=code. POST /api/drive/callback rejects bad code (400 'Could not exchange code with Google'), state mismatch (400 'OAuth state mismatch'), missing code (422 pydantic), and unauth (401). DELETE /api/drive/disconnect is idempotent (200 even when not connected). Frontend: nav-user-menu → menu-drive-connect visible with text 'Connect Google Drive' when not connected; clicking it calls GET /api/drive/connect and sets window.location.href to the returned Google URL (verified via response interception + actual navigation observed). /drive/callback page (data-testid drive-callback-page + drive-callback-msg): no-code path shows 'Authorization cancelled' and auto-redirects to /dashboard within ~2s; ?code=fake&state={user_id} POSTs to /api/drive/callback, gets 400, displays 'Could not exchange code with Google', then redirects to /dashboard. PostView as owner shows post-export-drive-btn (text 'Save to Drive' when not exported, 'Re-sync to Drive' once drive_file_id is set). When drive_web_view_link is set on the post (verified by manually patching the DB), post-drive-link renders next to the export button with href=https://drive.google.com/... and target=_blank. As anonymous (non-owner) viewer the export button is correctly hidden via the isOwner gate (user.user_id === post.author_id).
critical_code_review_comments: useDriveStatus.connect() sets window.location.href = data.authorization_url which immediately navigates away — busy/loading state inside the same page is moot. PostView.exportToDrive duplicates this behaviour. Both flows are correct, just observe that in playwright the .json() body of the connect response may be unretrievable after navigation; tests should rely on response.status + intercept request URL.

drive_callback enforces state-vs-user_id when state is provided (correct), but if state is missing the callback silently proceeds — acceptable since the frontend always sends ?state={user_id}, but defense-in-depth would be to require state. Not blocking.

_drive_service_for raises HTTPException(400, 'Google Drive is not connected for this account') — matches the contract. _find_or_create_folder runs a sync google client call inside an async route — works but blocks the event loop on each export. For 1 user that's fine; for a busier app consider asyncio.to_thread() around the service calls.

export-drive endpoint embeds post.title verbatim into the HTML <title> and <h1> without escaping — if a user puts < or > characters in their title, the generated Drive HTML breaks. Apply html.escape() on post.title and post.created_at. Low priority since titles are short user strings, but worth fixing.
DriveCallback.jsx uses processed.useRef to gate single-fire under React 18 StrictMode — good. Redirect timeouts (1100/1800/2000 ms) all under 2s — meets spec.
Frontend hook useDriveStatus refreshes status on user change only; if a user disconnects in another tab the navbar will stay stale until reload. Not blocking for phase 3.

Navbar: when connected, the menu item label 'Drive connected · disconnect' is a single MenuItem that triggers disconnect on click. Users expecting a label-only row might disconnect accidentally. Consider splitting into a label + a destructive sub-item or adding an AlertDialog confirm before revoking.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of testable flows — interactive Google OAuth login itself is not testable headlessly per Google policy

seed_data_creation: 1 TEST_drive_* user registered for cross-user 403 check (left in DB as before). 1 TEST_Drive_Export post created and deleted within fixture. Drive credentials never written (full OAuth not testable). Manually set + then unset drive_web_view_link on post_0c37317ad5ff to verify UI render of post-drive-link — DB now reverted to original state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full Google OAuth round-trip is NOT headlessly testable (Google blocks automated browser sign-ins). The construction of the authorization URL, the callback contract, and the export gating logic are fully covered by curl/pytest. For visual states of post-drive-link, manually patch db.posts.{drive_web_view_link, drive_file_id} on a demo post then reload PostView. Backend stores Drive tokens in db.drive_credentials keyed by user_id; export endpoint creates/updates HTML inside a 'The Tani Journal' folder (auto-created on first export). Frontend uses window.location.href for the OAuth redirect — playwright tests that need the response body after the click should use page.expect_response BEFORE click and check response.status only (body fetch may fail post-navigation). isOwner gate in PostView (user?.user_id === post.author_id) reliably hides the export button for anonymous + other users. Cookies are httpOnly so page.context.clear_cookies() works but you must reload after clearing — clearing mid-page does not re-render React.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional issues found. Two test-script-side hiccups were observed and worked around: (a) Response.json() after window.location.href change fails with 'No resource with given identifier' because the navigation tore down the network response stream — switched to checking response.status only. (b) Initial non-owner check incorrectly reused a stale page state from after the OAuth-redirect click; rerunning with explicit context.clear_cookies() + storage clear + fresh navigation confirmed isOwner gate works correctly.
